title: CI flake: head /dev/kmsg: EIO
body:
Very rare, seen three times this year:
```
[+0430s] • [FAILED] [1.443 seconds]
         Podman run device
           [It] podman run device test
         
           # podman [options] run -q --security-opt label=disable --device /dev/kmsg quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest test -c /dev/kmsg
           # podman [options] run -q --security-opt label=disable --device /dev/kmsg --cap-add SYS_ADMIN,SYSLOG quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest head -n 1 /dev/kmsg
           head: /dev/kmsg: I/O error
```
I don't see anything wrong with the test, and doubt that it's podman's fault. I fully expect this issue to languish a few months then get closed. That's OK, I just need a bucket for my flake catalog, because with ginkgo flakes retries disabled we're going to see this one in production.


* fedora-39 : int podman fedora-39 root host sqlite
    * [02-14-2024 14:36](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6152980831928320/html/int-podman-fedora-39-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-device-podman-run-device-test--1) in Podman run device podman run device test
* fedora-40 : int podman fedora-40 root container sqlite
  * PR #23846
    * [09-05 14:11](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5853901012860928/html/int-podman-fedora-40-root-container-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-device-podman-run-device-test--1) in Podman run device podman run device test
* rawhide : int remote rawhide root host sqlite [remote]
    * [03-05-2024 20:19](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5606311103037440/html/int-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-device-podman-run-device-test--1) in Podman run device podman run device test

| x | x | x | x | x | x |
| ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| int(3) | podman(2) | fedora-40(1) | root(3) | host(2) | sqlite(3) |
|  | remote(1) | rawhide(1) |  | container(1) |  |
|  |  | fedora-39(1) |  |  |  |

comments.body: No idea what triggers in EIO there but thinking out loud we could just use a different device I presume?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Still happening: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4587445502607360/html/int-remote-fedora-42-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-device-podman-run-device-test--1

@giuseppe Do you know why reading kmsg can return EIO? Is there a better device we should use instead or is there any need for us checking is we can read this at all? Maybe we should just drop it?

The `head` command present in the alpine image translates ESPIPE to EIO, so the root reason seems to be  some other process writing to the ring buffer while `head` tries to read from its open file descriptor.

The reproducer is quite easy:

```
# podman run -ti --rm -q --security-opt label=disable --device /dev/kmsg --cap-add SYS_ADMIN,SYSLOG quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest sh -c 'while true; do head -n1 /dev/kmsg || break; done'
```

then run from another terminal:

```
# while true; do echo garbage > /dev/kmsg ; done
```

Not sure what could be more reliable.  I am not sure we need to read from it, the security checks are done at `open` time, so we could just use `head -n0`.

If you are fine with this solution, I can open a PR
opened a PR so we can comment there: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26207
